max_achievable.explanation: Three criteria (output-exists, admission-count, discharge-count) have testPattern regex mismatches in the evaluation framework — they match describe block names but the JUnit XML parser extracts test case names. These criteria always score 0 regardless of skill quality.

changes_made: Reduced skill from 270 lines (verbose original) to ~100 lines optimized for qwen3-30b
Removed Python regex patterns, implementation algorithm, drug list, ICD-10 codes — model has this knowledge built-in
Added explicit JSON validity reminder (IMPORTANT: write complete, valid JSON)
Added concrete output format examples for entities.json and reconciliation.json
Added reconciliation algorithm as numbered steps for smaller model to follow
Added worked example showing correct reconciliation output including switched vs discontinued distinction
Emphasized switched is mutually exclusive with discontinued/new_meds with bold IMPORTANT callout
Added negation examples covering 'or' conjunction (denies nausea or vomiting → both negated)
key_findings: Baseline verbose skill (270 lines) caused qwen3-30b to produce malformed JSON (missing closing brace) for medication-reconciliation
Smaller models benefit from concise skills with worked examples over comprehensive reference material
Model consistently dispatches subagents for reconciliation task — worked example helps it produce correct output regardless
Note-extraction task is well-handled by qwen3-30b with minimal guidance — consistent 90/100 across all runs
Framework bug: JUnit XML parser extracts testcase name attributes but testPatterns match describe block classnames, causing 3 criteria to always score 0
